title: 🗿Lossless Context Management — Overcoming Context Rot In LLMs
author: Mayur Jain
text:
Member-only story
🗿Lossless Context Management — Overcoming Context Rot In LLMs
LLMs are limited by the number of tokens they can hold in their heads, so what’s next?
Introduction
Lately, the Context Window has become the new buzzword that everyone in AI is obsessed with. Whether it is ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini, everyone is fighting to see who can remember the most tokens. But here is the thing, just because an LLM (Large Language Model) can fit 1 million tokens in its head doesn’t mean it actually understands or remembers everything perfectly. Often, as the conversation gets longer, the model starts to get confused, and a problem the experts call context rot begins to appear.
Today, we’ll deep-dive into Lossless Context Management (LCM), a technique that aims to address context rot. Developed by the folks at Voltropy, this is a new way to handle LLM memory that doesn’t just throw data at the model but manages it with proper levels of organization.
Let’s get into the core ideas of LCM and see why it might just be the future of how AI agents work.
What is Lossless Context Management (LCM)
At its core, LCM is a deterministic architecture for LLM memory. In simple words, it’s a smart filing system for…
